results.title: Russia in Europe
results.brief_description: A survey of European Russia, west of the Ural Mountains. Shows the topography, living conditions, agriculture, waterways and chief cities of this area including Yalta on the Black Sea. The Communist form of government is discussed briefly. Heavy industry has expanded under the five and seven year plans, but the standard of living is lower than in many Western countries.

results.title: Russia beneath the sputniks
results.brief_description: In 1957 the Soviet Union put the first satellite into space, and since then has achieved other spectacular “firsts” in space. But Soviet scientists are also tackling other problems of more immediate consequence to its 200 million population. The opening up of the frozen deserts of the Arctic Circle and the discovery of minerals there; the chemical compounding of foods; and the use of computers particularly in the field of hospital surgery and medical research are a part of the quiet revolution in progress behind the closed doors of the Soviet Union.

results.title: Rushing to sunshine (Seoul diaries)
results.brief_description: A sequel to Hoaas’ “Pyongyang diaries”, “Rushing to sunshine” continues the filmmaker’s personal diaristic exploration of the social and political realities of life in North and South Korea. Drawing on material she gathered over a two year period from March 1998 to March 2000, a period of great economic upheaval and social change in South Korea, Hoaas’ reflects on the country’s attempts at reconciliation with North Korea motivated by Kim Dae Jung’s ‘Sunshine Policy.’ Combining her personal insights with the stories of former political prisoners, student dissidents, academics and people on the street, Hoaas uncovers the uncomfortable paradoxes that face a society caught between the Government’s new open door policy and the remnants of a cold war anti-Communist mindset. In English and Korean with English subtitles.

results.title: Rush hour
results.brief_description: From 08:20 to 09:20 the commuters pour into London’s Waterloo Station. Using speed photography and the sound of the clicking clock, this hour has been effectively compressed into three minutes adding extra momentum to the rush hour at Waterloo Station.

results.title: Rural nurse: a pilot project in El Salvador
results.brief_description: The story of the setting up and organisation of a demonstration area as part of the El Salvador Nursing Program, which is a plan to tackle simultaneously problems of food, health, education and living standards.

results.title: Rural life of Mexico
results.brief_description: A study of the life of isolated communities in the mountains of Mexico. Living largely by agriculture and using methods of cultivation which have scarcely changed during the centuries, these Mexicans still wear their regional costumes unselfconsciously and dance at fiesta time their traditional dances.

results.title: Rural life in Western Ireland: Irish children
results.brief_description: A brother and sister in rural western Ireland help on the farm and in the house; they help to dig turf from the bog, and, together with their family, enjoy a picnic.

results.title: Rural Japan: helping to feed a nation
results.brief_description: Shows the everyday life of the average Japanese farmer, his home and surroundings. Also shows the effects of recent changes on his way of life - the cultivation of plants under hot house conditions, the use of tractors instead of hard manual labour and how rural life has changed since the War with the influence of America.

results.title: Rupert Bunny: colour, mood & the senses
results.creator_credit: Eugene Schlusser, Reg McLean and Lion's Den Productions
results.brief_description: A detailed documentary examining the works and background of Australian artist, Rupert Bunny, focussing on the factors which made his work so admired by both collectors and fellow artists. Born in Melbourne, Bunny was one of the few Australian artists painting between the Romantic and Modernist periods to achieve international success by selling works all over the world. While his subjects ranged from mythological characters, seascapes, fairytales, and the spiritual, they all shared a common thread in that their purpose was to appeal to all of the senses. The concept of synthaesthesia - that one sense can be evoked by another - is strikingly evident in the sensuously elegant content of his paintings. A number of art critics offer their opinions on why they believe Bunny is one of the most masterly painters that Australia has ever produced.

results.title: Rupert and the Frog Song
results.brief_description: Features Paul McCartney’s “Rupert and the Frog” song. Also Linda McCartney’s “The Oriental Nightfish” and delightful award winning “Seaside Woman”. Presented by Paul and Linda McCartney. Rated: G
